title: Quiz: Electronic and Semiconductor Materials
description: Test your understanding of semiconductor materials modeling

questions.question: What primarily determines carrier mobility in semiconductors?
questions.explanation: Mobility μ = eτ/m* depends on effective mass (m*) and scattering time (τ). Light carriers (small m*) and weak scattering (long τ) give high mobility. GaAs has higher mobility than Si partly due to its lighter electron effective mass.

questions.question: In defect calculations, what does the charge transition level ε(q/q') represent?

options.text: The band gap of the material
The Fermi level where defect changes charge state
The work function of the material

questions.explanation: The charge transition level ε(q/q') is the Fermi level position where the defect formation energies of two charge states are equal. Above this level, charge q' is stable; below it, charge q is stable.

questions.question: Why is GaN p-type doping more challenging than n-type?

options.text: GaN has a direct band gap
Mg acceptors have deep levels and are compensated
GaN is unstable under p-type conditions
Holes have infinite effective mass in GaN

questions.explanation: Mg acceptors in GaN have relatively deep ionization energies (~200 meV) and are often compensated by native defects. This makes achieving high p-type conductivity difficult compared to n-type (Si doping).

questions.question: What type of band alignment is desired for efficient charge separation in solar cells?
questions.explanation: Type II (staggered) alignment separates electrons and holes to different materials, preventing recombination. Electrons go to the material with lower conduction band, holes to the one with higher valence band.

questions.question: Graphene is a good semiconductor for digital logic applications due to its high mobility.
questions.explanation: False. While graphene has extremely high mobility, it lacks a band gap (it's a semimetal). Digital logic requires on/off switching, which needs a band gap. Graphene transistors can't be turned off effectively.

questions.question: Which corrections are needed for charged defect calculations? (Select all)
questions.explanation: Charged defect supercell calculations require corrections for: image charge interactions across periodic boundaries, potential alignment to bulk far from the defect, and band filling effects when shallow defects spill into bands. vdW is generally not needed for point defects.
